description: General Inspection Level I - Reduced discrimination. Use when less discrimination is needed or when testing is destructive.
General Inspection Level II - Standard discrimination. Most commonly used general inspection level for routine inspections.
General Inspection Level III - Increased discrimination. Use when greater discrimination is needed for critical items.
Special Inspection Level S-1 - Very small sample sizes. Used for expensive or destructive testing where minimum sample size is required.
Special Inspection Level S-2 - Small sample sizes. Used for costly or destructive testing with slightly larger samples than S-1.
Special Inspection Level S-3 - Medium sample sizes. Used for moderately destructive testing or when S-1/S-2 are insufficient.
Special Inspection Level S-4 - Large sample sizes for special cases. Used when larger samples are needed for special destructive testing.
